source_rationale: RGS5 is a pericyte/perivascular marker highly expressed in DDX4 AB+ cells, suggesting a perivascular identity for this sorted population
ACTA2 (alpha smooth muscle actin) is a canonical marker of smooth muscle/perivascular cells enriched in this population
TAGLN (transgelin) is a smooth muscle marker consistent with perivascular cell identity
MCAM (CD146) is a pericyte surface marker used to identify perivascular progenitor cells
NOTCH3 is a key signaling receptor in pericytes and vascular smooth muscle cells
PDGFRB is a canonical pericyte receptor essential for pericyte recruitment and signaling
MYH11 is a smooth muscle myosin heavy chain specific to contractile smooth muscle/perivascular cells
PLN (phospholamban) is expressed in smooth muscle cells and regulates calcium handling
CD9 is a tetraspanin enriched on the surface of perivascular and germ cell-associated populations
DCN (decorin) is a hallmark extracellular matrix proteoglycan of ovarian stromal fibroblasts
PDGFRA is a canonical marker of mesenchymal stromal/fibroblast cells in the ovary
COL1A1 encodes type I collagen, a major structural protein produced by stromal fibroblasts
SERPINE2 is a serine protease inhibitor expressed in ovarian stromal cells involved in tissue remodeling
FOXL2 is a key transcription factor for ovarian somatic cell identity and granulosa/stromal differentiation
CLDN11 is a tight junction protein expressed in ovarian stromal cells
STAR (steroidogenic acute regulatory protein) is essential for steroidogenesis in ovarian somatic cells
CXCL12 is a chemokine produced by ovarian stromal cells important for niche signaling
TCF21 is a transcription factor marking stromal/mesenchymal progenitors in the ovary
VWF (von Willebrand factor) is a canonical endothelial cell marker involved in hemostasis
CLDN5 is a tight junction protein specific to endothelial cells
CDH5 (VE-cadherin) is a definitive endothelial-specific adhesion molecule
CD34 is a well-established marker of endothelial and hematopoietic progenitor cells
KDR (VEGFR2) is the primary VEGF receptor on endothelial cells, essential for angiogenesis
EGFL7 is an endothelial-specific secreted factor important for vascular development
EMCN (endomucin) is a sialomucin specifically expressed on endothelial cells
FLT1 (VEGFR1) is a VEGF receptor expressed on endothelial cells
PTPRB (VE-PTP) is an endothelial-specific receptor tyrosine phosphatase
SOX17 is a transcription factor critical for arterial endothelial cell specification
TIE1 is an endothelial-specific receptor tyrosine kinase involved in vascular development
AMH (anti-Müllerian hormone) is a canonical marker of granulosa cells in growing follicles
FOXL2 is the master transcription factor for granulosa cell identity and differentiation
INHA (inhibin alpha) is produced by granulosa cells and is a key reproductive hormone subunit
HSD17B1 is a steroidogenic enzyme highly expressed in granulosa cells for estrogen biosynthesis
AMHR2 (AMH receptor type 2) is specifically expressed in granulosa cells mediating AMH signaling
KRT18 is an epithelial cytokeratin characteristic of granulosa cells
WFDC2 (HE4) is highly expressed in granulosa cells and is a known ovarian biomarker
WT1 is a transcription factor expressed in granulosa cells essential for ovarian development
HEY2 is a Notch target transcription factor involved in granulosa cell differentiation
PTGDS (prostaglandin D2 synthase) is highly expressed in granulosa cells of the ovary
DSP (desmoplakin) marks the epithelial junctions characteristic of granulosa cell layers
TNNI3 is unexpectedly highly upregulated in granulosa cells and serves as a distinguishing marker
PTPRC (CD45) is the pan-leukocyte marker expressed on all immune cells
CD14 is a monocyte/macrophage co-receptor for LPS, marking myeloid immune cells
TYROBP (DAP12) is an immune signaling adaptor broadly expressed in myeloid and NK cells
AIF1 (IBA1) is a macrophage/microglia marker involved in immune cell activation
CD69 is an early activation marker on T cells and other immune cells
FCER1G is an Fc receptor gamma chain expressed on myeloid and NK immune cells
C1QA is a complement component produced by tissue-resident macrophages
LYZ (lysozyme) is a canonical myeloid cell marker with antimicrobial function
CD52 is a GPI-anchored glycoprotein broadly expressed on lymphocytes and monocytes
NKG7 is expressed in NK and cytotoxic T cells, marking cytotoxic immune populations
CD14 is the canonical monocyte surface marker and LPS co-receptor
CD163 is a scavenger receptor marking tissue-resident monocytes/macrophages
C1QA is a complement component highly expressed by tissue monocytes/macrophages
C1QB is a complement subunit produced by monocyte-derived macrophages
C1QC completes the C1q complement complex characteristic of tissue macrophages
LYZ (lysozyme) is a canonical monocyte/macrophage marker with antimicrobial activity
CSF1R is the M-CSF receptor essential for monocyte/macrophage differentiation and survival
FCGR2A (CD32) is an Fc gamma receptor expressed on monocytes for immune complex recognition
FOLR2 is a folate receptor marking tissue-resident macrophages
LYVE1 marks a subset of tissue-resident perivascular macrophages
MAFB is a transcription factor driving monocyte-to-macrophage differentiation
MS4A6A is a membrane-spanning protein enriched in monocytes and macrophages
CTSS (cathepsin S) is a cysteine protease involved in MHC-II antigen processing in monocytes
ZP3 (zona pellucida glycoprotein 3) is an oocyte-specific structural protein of the zona pellucida
FIGLA is an oocyte-specific transcription factor essential for folliculogenesis
NLRP5 is a maternal-effect gene specifically expressed in oocytes
DPPA3 (Stella) is a germ cell-specific gene essential for oocyte epigenetic reprogramming
ZAR1 is an oocyte-specific RNA-binding protein essential for early embryonic development
SYCP3 is a synaptonemal complex protein marking meiotic germ cells/oocytes
LHX8 is a transcription factor essential for oocyte survival and early follicle development
SOHLH2 is a germ cell-specific transcription factor required for oogenesis
TUBB8 is an oocyte-specific beta-tubulin essential for meiotic spindle assembly
KPNA7 is an oocyte-enriched importin alpha required for nuclear transport during oogenesis
STAG3 is a meiosis-specific cohesin subunit essential for oocyte chromosome segregation
FOXR1 is a forkhead transcription factor with oocyte-enriched expression
RGS5 is the most established pericyte marker, a GTPase-activating protein enriched in perivascular cells
ACTA2 (alpha-SMA) is a canonical smooth muscle/pericyte contractile protein
TAGLN (SM22) is a smooth muscle-specific protein marking perivascular cells
MYH11 is a smooth muscle myosin heavy chain defining contractile perivascular/smooth muscle cells
PDGFRB is the canonical pericyte receptor for PDGF-BB signaling in vascular mural cells
MCAM (CD146) is a pericyte surface marker used for isolation of perivascular cells
NOTCH3 is essential for pericyte/vascular smooth muscle cell maturation and identity
PLN (phospholamban) regulates calcium signaling in smooth muscle/perivascular cells
MYL9 is a myosin regulatory light chain specific to smooth muscle/perivascular cells
CNN1 (calponin) is a smooth muscle-specific actin-binding protein marking perivascular cells
DCN (decorin) is a proteoglycan abundantly produced by stromal fibroblasts in the ovary
PDGFRA is a canonical fibroblast/stromal cell receptor marking mesenchymal populations
COL1A1 is a major collagen gene defining extracellular matrix-producing stromal fibroblasts
COL1A2 encodes type I collagen alpha-2 chain, a hallmark of fibroblast/stromal identity
TCF21 is a transcription factor marking ovarian stromal/mesenchymal progenitor cells
OGN (osteoglycin) is a small leucine-rich proteoglycan characteristic of stromal fibroblasts
CXCL12 (SDF-1) is a chemokine secreted by stromal cells to maintain the tissue niche
LUM (lumican) is a proteoglycan produced by stromal fibroblasts in connective tissue
STAR is a steroidogenic enzyme expressed in ovarian theca/stromal cells
FN1 (fibronectin) is a major extracellular matrix glycoprotein produced by stromal fibroblasts
CLDN11 is a tight junction protein expressed in ovarian stromal cells
CD3D is a component of the T cell receptor complex, a definitive T cell marker
CD3E is a core T cell receptor signaling subunit specific to T lymphocytes
CD8A marks cytotoxic T cells and is a canonical co-receptor for MHC class I
CCL5 (RANTES) is a chemokine highly expressed by activated and memory T cells
GZMK (granzyme K) is a serine protease marking effector/memory CD8+ T cells
GZMA (granzyme A) is a cytotoxic granule protease characteristic of T and NK cells
IL7R (CD127) is a key cytokine receptor marking naive and memory T cells
PTPRC (CD45) is the pan-leukocyte marker essential for T cell receptor signaling
CD2 is a T cell surface glycoprotein involved in T cell activation and adhesion
NKG7 is a cytotoxic granule membrane protein expressed in activated cytotoxic T cells
LCK is a Src family kinase essential for T cell receptor signaling
KLRB1 (CD161) is a C-type lectin receptor expressed on T cell subsets including NKT cells
IFNG (interferon-gamma) is the signature cytokine of Th1 and cytotoxic T cells
